mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The positive return signal reverses to negative during mid-cycle expansions, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power outside late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where monthly CPI growth surpasses the 0.4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for payroll growth against 60-day forward returns consistently remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth surpasses 2.0 percent while personal consumption expenditures simultaneously accelerate, the combined demand shock validates earnings upgrades and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive 20-day returns in over 65% of triggered instances across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal supply-side contraction that overwhelms demand buffers, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during late-cycle stagnation phases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns do not materialize in at least 70% of late-cycle periods following two consecutive industrial production declines.
